lines: Let's begin by settling in. [long pause] You don't need to fix anything right now. [long pause] You don't need to solve anything. [long pause] For these next few minutes, the only task is to be here.
Start with your breath. [long pause] We'll use a pattern called box breathing — it's simple, and it gives your nervous system something steady to hold onto.
Breathe in through your nose for four counts... [long pause] [long pause] one... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four.
Hold gently... [long pause] [long pause] one... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four.
Exhale slowly through your mouth... [long pause] [long pause] one... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four.
And hold at the bottom... [long pause] [long pause] one... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four.
Again. [long pause] In... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four.
Hold... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four.
Out... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four.
Hold... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four.
One more round. [long pause] In... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four.
Hold... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four.
Out... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four.
Hold... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four.
Good. [long pause] Let the box pattern soften. [long pause] Breathe naturally now, but keep that gentle steadiness. [long pause] Your body is already slowing down. [long pause] Trust it.

I want to start with your body, because anxiety doesn't just live in your mind. [long pause] It takes up residence in your flesh. [long pause] It tightens things. [long pause] It braces things. [long pause] And sometimes, before you can think your way through worry, you need to feel your way into it. [long pause] You need to know where it lives.
So let's do that. [long pause] A slow scan. [long pause] No rushing.

Bring your attention to your feet. [long pause] Just notice them. [long pause] The soles pressing against the floor, or tucked beneath you — whatever position they're in. [long pause] Feel the contact. [long pause] The weight. [long pause] The temperature. [long pause] There's nothing to fix here. [long pause] Just arrive in your feet.

Now move up to your legs. [long pause] Your shins. [long pause] Your knees. [long pause] Your thighs. [long pause] Are they tight? [long pause] Restless? [long pause] Heavy? [long pause] Numb? [long pause] There's no wrong answer. [long pause] Just notice what's actually here — not what you think should be here.

Now your stomach. [long pause] This is where many people hold anxiety — a knot, a churning, a hollowness, a fluttering. [long pause] Some of you felt it the moment I said the word. [long pause] Don't try to relax it. [long pause] Just place your attention there, the way you'd place a hand on a friend's shoulder. [long pause] Gently. [long pause] Just... [long pause] [long pause] I see you. [long pause] I know you're there.

Breathe into your stomach now. [long pause] Imagine your breath is warm, and you're directing it there. [long pause] Not to fix the feeling. [long pause] Just to accompany it. [long pause] Inhale toward the tension. [long pause] Exhale, and let whatever wants to soften... [long pause] [long pause] soften. [long pause] Whatever doesn't... [long pause] [long pause] that's fine too.

Your chest. [long pause] This is the other home of anxiety — tightness, compression, like someone's sitting on you. [long pause] Or a fluttering behind the breastbone. [long pause] Or a heaviness. [long pause] What do you find? [long pause] Just notice. [long pause] Breathe around it.

Your throat. [long pause] Anxiety often grips here — a lump, a constriction, a sense of words trapped or breath narrowed. [long pause] Pay attention. [long pause] What's the quality of the tightness? [long pause] Does it have a shape? [long pause] A temperature?

Your jaw. [long pause] Are your teeth clenched? [long pause] Even slightly? [long pause] Let your mouth come open just a fraction. [long pause] Teeth apart. [long pause] Tongue resting against the roof of your mouth, loose.

Your forehead. [long pause] Your temples. [long pause] The space behind your eyes. [long pause] Is there a furrow? [long pause] A pressing? [long pause] A band of tension? [long pause] Notice it. [long pause] Breathe.

Your shoulders. [long pause] Are they crept up toward your ears? [long pause] Let them drop. [long pause] Not forcefully. [long pause] Just... [long pause] [long pause] stop holding them up for a moment.

Now take one slow breath — a big one — and as you exhale, let everything soften at once. [long pause] Not forcing relaxation. [long pause] Not demanding peace. [long pause] Just... [long pause] [long pause] inviting your body to let go of whatever it's willing to let go of right now.

Good. [long pause] Whatever you found in that scan — tension, tightness, nothing, everything — that's honest information. [long pause] That's your body telling you the truth about how you're carrying this day.
Before we go further, I want to say something that might be important for you to hear, and I want you to receive it slowly.

Anxiety is not a failure of faith. [long pause] Worry is not disobedience. [long pause] The saints worried. [long pause] The psalmists panicked. [long pause] David cried out from caves. [long pause] Elijah sat under a tree and asked God to let him die. [long pause] Jesus himself — Jesus, the Son of God — sweated blood in a garden the night before his death. [long pause] His body shook with dread. [long pause] He asked for the cup to be taken away.

If you've ever felt ashamed of your anxiety — as though a real Christian wouldn't feel this way, as though stronger faith would make the worry stop — let that go. [long pause] Right now. [long pause] Let it go. [long pause] God is not disappointed in you for being afraid. [long pause] He made your nervous system. [long pause] He knows how it works. [long pause] He knows the cost of being human, because he paid it himself.

Now. [long pause] Listen to these words from Jesus. [long pause] Don't hear them as a command. [long pause] Don't hear them as a rebuke. [long pause] Hear them as an invitation — spoken gently, by someone who knows exactly what fear tastes like.

"Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or drink; or about your body, what you will wear. [long pause] Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothes? [long pause] Look at the birds of the air; they do not sow or reap or store away in barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them. [long pause] Are you not much more valuable than they? [long pause] Can any one of you by worrying add a single hour to your life?"
Matthew six, verses twenty-five through twenty-seven.

Jesus asks a practical question here. [long pause] Has worry ever added an hour to your life? [long pause] Has it ever actually solved the problem it promised to solve? [long pause] Be honest with yourself.

God, I bring you this worry. [long pause] Not because I've figured out how to stop worrying — I haven't. [long pause] Not because I'm strong enough to just hand it over — some days I'm not. [long pause] But because you told me I could come to you with it. [long pause] You said come, and I'm coming.

I don't ask you to take the anxiety away. [long pause] I've asked that before and sometimes it stays. [long pause] So instead I ask: be in it with me. [long pause] Steady my breath when my chest is tight. [long pause] Loosen my jaw when I'm clenching against the future. [long pause] Remind me, in the moments I forget, that I am not carrying this alone.

I know the birds don't worry, and I know I'm worth more than they are. [long pause] But I'm not a bird, Lord. [long pause] I'm a person with a mind that races and a body that braces and a heart that sometimes forgets how to trust. [long pause] Meet me there. [long pause] In the racing. [long pause] In the bracing. [long pause] In the forgetting.

Teach me to hold my worries loosely. [long pause] To stop gripping the future so hard that I miss the ground beneath my feet right now. [long pause] And when the worry comes back — because it will, we both know it will — help me remember this moment. [long pause] This breath. [long pause] Your presence. [long pause] This invitation to be still and know.
Not because I'm strong enough. [long pause] Because you are.

Take one more slow breath. [long pause] Feel your body in this chair, on this floor, in this room. [long pause] Feel the air on your skin. [long pause] Hear the sounds around you. [long pause] The world is still here. [long pause] You are still here.
You are here. [long pause] You are not alone. [long pause] And this moment — this one breath, right now — is enough.

When you're ready, gently open your eyes. [long pause] Move slowly. [long pause] There's no rush. [long pause] Carry whatever stillness you found with you — not as a demand on yourself, but as a gift. [long pause] Even if it's small. [long pause] Even if it's fragile. [long pause] It's yours. [long pause] And you can come back to it.
